CapGainsLossTxInvstIncmGrp.PropertyDesc: 39,660 SHS OF UTILITIES SEL SECT SPDR FUND
35,340 SHS OF UTILITIES SEL SECT SPDR FUND

CompensationOfHghstPdCntrctGrp.ServiceTypeTxt: CONSULTING FOR REAL ESTATE, PLANNING AND COMMUNITY OUTREACH

SumOfProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Description1Txt: ON AUGUST 31, 2021 AND DECEMBER 16, 2021, THE ORGANIZATION CONTRIBUTED LAND AND BUILDINGS TO BE USED FOR THE DEVELOPMENT OF AFFORDABLE HOUSING IN THE CITY HEIGHTS AREA OF SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA (THE "CONTRIBUTED REAL ESTATE"), TO THE WAKELAND HOUSING & DEVELOPMENT CORP.
SumOfProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Description2Txt: IN CONNECTION WITH THE ABOVE CONTRIBUTED REAL ESTATE (SEE STATEMENT 16), THE ORGANIZATION ENTERED INTO TWO AGREEMENTS TO PROVIDE PROGRAM RELATED INVESTMENT ("PRI") LOANS TO WAKELAND HOUSING & DEVELOPMENT CORP. THE PRI LOAN FOR THE REAL ESTATE CONTRIBUTION MADE ON AUGUST 31, 2021 IS NOT TO EXCEED $8,085,000 AND $4,042,500 OF THIS PRI WAS FUNDED ON AUGUST 31, 2021. (SEE STATEMENT 18 FOR THE PRI RELATED TO THE REAL ESTATE CONTRIBUTION MADE ON DECEMBER 16, 2021)

ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.FormAndInfoAndMaterialsTxt: WE ASK THAT ALL APPLICANTS SUBMIT AN INITIAL GRANT REQUEST. AFTER AN INTERNAL SCREENING PROCESS SOME APPLICANTS WILL BE ASKED TO SUBMIT A MORE COMPREHENSIVE GRANT REQUEST. THE INITIAL GRANT REQUEST MUST INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION: - THE ORGANIZATION'S BACKGROUND; AMOUNT REQUESTED; BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF SPECIFIC PROJECTS FOR WHICH FUNDING IS DESIRED; SPECIFIC DESCRIPTION OF HOW THIS GRANT SEEKS TO IMPROVE LIFE OPPORTUNITIES FOR YOUTH AND FAMILIES; ORGANIZATION ANNUAL BUDGET; PROGRAM OR PROJECT BUDGET; CONTACT INFORMATION FOR THE PERSON WHO SHOULD BE CONTACTED REGARDING THIS REQUEST; LIST OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS; AND THE 501(C)(3) DETERMINATION LETTER THE INITIAL GRANT REQUEST MUST BE SUBMITTED THROUGH OUR ONLINE SYSTEM.
ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.RestrictionsOnAwardsTxt: GRANTS MUST BE MADE TO NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS WITH 501(C)(3) STATUS OR GOVERNMENTAL UNITS SUCH AS PUBLIC SCHOOLS OR CITY DEPARTMENTS OR FOR INHERENTLY CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES; GRANTS ARE NOT MADE (I) TO ORGANIZATIONS WHOSE PRIMARY PURPOSE IS RELIGIOUS, OR (II) FOR PROPAGANDIZING, INFLUENCING LEGISLATION AND/OR ELECTIONS, PROMOTING VOTING REGISTRATION, FOR POLITICAL CANDIDATES, POLITICAL CAMPAIGNS OR ORGANIZATIONS ENGAGED IN POLITICAL ACTIVITIES; OR (III) TO FEDERAL APPEALS OR TO ORGANIZATIONS THAT COLLECT FUNDS FOR REDISTRIBUTION TO OTHER NON-PROFIT GROUPS.

AllOtherProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Desc: THE ORGANIZATION ENTERED INTO TWO AGREEMENTS TO PROVIDE PRI LOANS TO WAKELAND HOUSING & DEVELOPMENT CORP. THE PRI LOAN FOR THE REAL ESTATE CONTRIBUTION MADE ON DECEMBER 16, 2021 IS NOT TO EXCEED $14,250,000 AND $7,125,000 OF THIS PRI WAS FUNDED ON DECEMBER 16, 2021.

THE EAST BLOCK DEVELOPMENT IS A RESIDENTIAL AFFORDABLE HOUSING DEVELOPMENT PROJECT IN THE CITY HEIGHTS AREA OF SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA. DURING 2021, RELATED TO THE AFFORDABLE FAMILY HOUSING COMPONENT OF THE EAST BLOCK DEVELOPMENT, THE ORGANIZATION CONTRIBUTED AN ADDITIONAL $270,000.
IN ADDITION, THE ORGANIZATION COMMITTED TO SPEND $2,572,927 FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF PARKING WITHIN THE EAST BLOCK DEVELOPMENT, WHICH WAS FUNDED IN APRIL 2021.

ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.PurposeOfGrantTxt: THE CAPITAL ENDOWMENT GRANT MUST BE EXPENDED FOR CHARITABLE, SCIENTIFIC, LITERARY, OR EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES AS DEFINED UNDER INTERNAL REVENUE CODE 501(C)(3), AND MORE SPECIFICALLY LIMITED TO SUPPORT AND OPERATE THE AARON PRICE FELLOWS PROGRAM. THE PROGRAM PREPARES HIGHLY MOTIVATED AND DIVERSE SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA, PUBLIC HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS TO BE RESPONSIBLE, ENGAGED, AND CARING MEMBERS OF THEIR COMMUNITY BY FOSTERING FRIENDSHIPS BETWEEN FELLOWS FROM DIVERSE ETHNIC, RELIGIOUS, AND ECONOMIC BACKGROUNDS; TEACHING FELLOWS ABOUT GOVERNMENT, CULTURAL, BUSINESS, AND NON-PROFIT INSTITUTIONS; AND EXPOSING FELLOWS TO EXCITING CAREER OPPORTUNITIES. ANY FUNDS NOT SO EXPENDED OR MAINTAINED AS AN ENDOWMENT FOR THE BENEFIT OF THE AARON PRICE FELLOWS PROGRAM MUST BE RETURNED TO PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION. AS OF DECEMBER 31, 2021, $3,922,044 OF THE ORIGINAL GRANT OF $25,000,000 HAS BEEN EXPENDED, AND $988,159 OF THIS AMOUNT IS ATTRIBUTABLE TO THE CURRENT REPORTING PERIOD.
ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.ResultsOfVerificationTxt: THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION RECEIVED A REPORT FROM THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF THE AARON PRICE FELLOWS FOUNDATION DATED JANUARY 31, 2022 AS VERIFICATION OF THE ACTIVITIES OF THE AARON PRICE FELLOWS FOUNDATION.

GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: 1(A)(4): DURING THE REPORTING PERIOD, THE ORGANIZATION PAID MANAGEMENT FEES TO THE PRICE GROUP, LLC. CERTAIN MEMBERS OF THE PRICE GROUP, LLC ARE ALSO DIRECTORS AND OFFICERS OF THE ORGANIZATION. NO COMPENSATION IS PAID DIRECTLY FROM THE ORGANIZATION TO THE MEMBERS OF THE PRICE GROUP, LLC.INSTEAD, CERTAIN MEMBERS ARE COMPENSATED FROM THE LLC. THE MANAGEMENT SERVICES RENDERED TO THE ORGANIZATION WERE MADE ON A BASIS NO MORE FAVORABLE THAN THAT ON WHICH SUCH SERVICES ARE PROVIDED IN THE MARKET PLACE.SEE IRC SECTION 4941(D)(2)(D).

THE CITY HEIGHTS REAL ESTATE PROGRAM PROVIDES A SAFE, CLEAN, AND AFFORDABLE LIVING AND WORK ENVIRONMENT THROUGH THE ACQUISITION AND CONSTRUCTION OF SEVERAL REAL ESTATE PROJECTS IN THE CITY HEIGHTS AREA OF THE CITY OF SAN DIEGO, CA. SUCH PROJECTS INCLUDE THE CONSTRUCTION AND MANAGEMENT OF: A 116 UNIT APARTMENT COMPLEX; A SIX-STORY (119,000 SQ FOOT) OFFICE BUILDING; A MIXED-USE RESIDENTIAL (92 UNITS) AND RETAIL (20,000 SQ FOOT) PROJECT; AND VARIOUS PARCELS OF LAND HELD FOR FUTURE PROJECTS. THE APRENDER Y CRECER PROGRAM PROVIDES SCHOOL SUPPLIES TO STUDENTS AND TEACHERS AND CLEANING SUPPLIES TO SCHOOLS IN VARIOUS CITIES IN CENTRAL AMERICA.THE CITY HEIGHTS INITIATIVE IS A PARTNERSHIP BETWEEN PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION AND PUBLIC AND NON-PROFIT AGENCIES IN THE CITY HEIGHTS AREA OF SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA TO PROVIDE OPPORTUNITIES FOR CITY HEIGHTS YOUTH AND FAMILIES TO PROSPER. THE INITIATIVE TAKES A HOLISTIC APPROACH TO IMPROVING RESIDENTS OF CITY HEIGHTS QUALITY OF LIFE THROUGH ACCESS TO HEALTH AND SOCIAL SERVICES, QUALITY EDUCATION, JOBS, LEGAL REPRESENTATION AND SAFE NEIGHBORHOODS.CARDINALS INTERACT IS A MENTORING AND TUTORING PROGRAM RUN BY PRICE PHILANTHROPIES FOUNDATION FOR STUDENTS AT HOOVER HIGH SCHOOL IN SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA. ITS MISSION IS TO HELP STUDENTS REACH THEIR MAXIMUM POTENTIAL ACADEMICALLY AND PERSONALLY AND ASSIST THEM IN DEVELOPING SKILLS IN THEAREAS OF TEAMWORK, SELF-ESTEEM, DECISION MAKING AND COMMUNICATION.THE EXPENSES LISTED IN THE SUMMARY OF DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES (PART VIII-A) INCLUDE ALL EXPENSES RELATED TO THE PROGRAMS LISTED, INCLUDING SALARIES AND BENEFITS.
